exercises.prompt: Which triplet is ordered familier → courant → soutenu?
« J'sais pas, sa bagnole est naze. » — which register?
exercises.explanation: Dropped ne (j'sais pas), bagnole, naze — all markers of spoken slang.

exercises.template: Le maire est hostile au projet ; son adjointe y est {{b1}}.
exercises.explanation: Hostile ↔ favorable — the formal antonym pair.

exercises.prompt: Why is « Si vous m'élisez, je serai aux petits oignons ! » funny in a campaign speech?

options.text: Slang clashes with the formal situation — a register mismatch

exercises.explanation: The comic effect comes from familiar idiom inside a formal speech — register mismatch.

exercises.template: Les idées abondent, mais les moyens {{b1}}.
exercises.explanation: Abonder ↔ manquer: ideas abound, means are lacking.

exercises.prompt: Match each formal word to its antonym.
Put the words in order: "We ask you to publish this clarification."
Listen. Which register, and how do you know?
exercises.transcript: Puis-je vous demander de rectifier ces propos dans votre prochaine édition ?

options.text: Soutenu — inversion (puis-je) and formal vocabulary (rectifier, propos)

exercises.explanation: Puis-je (inversion) + rectifier ces propos = textbook soutenu.

exercises.prompt: Read the right-of-reply letter and answer both questions.
exercises.passageFr: DROIT DE RÉPONSE — À la suite de votre article « Un quartier à l'abandon », nous tenons à rectifier plusieurs propos. Contrairement à vos affirmations, la gestion de notre association est transparente : nos comptes, loin d'être opaques, sont publiés chaque année. Le ton belliqueux de votre enquête nous a surpris ; notre réponse, elle, se veut pacifique. Nous vous prions de publier cette mise au point.
exercises.passageEn: RIGHT OF REPLY — Following your article "A neighbourhood abandoned", we wish to set several statements straight. Contrary to your claims, our association's management is transparent: our accounts, far from opaque, are published every year. The warlike tone of your investigation surprised us; our reply intends to be peaceful. We ask you to publish this clarification.

questions.prompt: What do the writers say about their accounts?

options.text: Far from opaque — published every year

questions.prompt: Which antonym pair structures the last argument?

exercises.prompt: Your friend texts: « On va au ciné ? » You reply to your boss instead. Which version fits?

options.text: On va au ciné ou quoi ?

exercises.explanation: To a superior → soutenu: conditional + inversion, full word cinéma.

exercises.prompt: Listen, then choose what it means.
exercises.transcript: Ma bagnole est en panne — ma voiture ne démarre plus.

options.text: My wheels broke down — my car won't start.
May I ask you to correct these statements?
Ideas abound, but means are lacking.

exercises.prompt: What does « un registre de langue » mean?
exercises.explanation: un registre de langue = a language register.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word (from this unit's vocabulary).
exercises.explanation: « Nous tenons à rectifier ces propos. » — to correct / set straight.

Put the words in order: “May I ask you to correct these statements? (formal)”
Speaking practice — listen, then say the sentence aloud and record yourself:

exercises.targetEn: My wheels broke down — my car won't start. (two registers)
